test: WARM libp2p vs raw-TCP A/B on a copy/retrieval workload — the REAL transport speed (closes the 2.86 cold-floor open thread)
ring: N=4 scattered US 4090s, even 9-layer split: MN(head)->IL->MI->TX (Minnesota/Illinois/Michigan/Texas, 4 distinct hosts), 48GB WA spare
workload: copy/retrieval — reproduce a ~31.7k-token codebase file (db.ts) VERBATIM; model-free n-gram spec-decode engages (mean accept 5.69/round, g=6.69 tok/traversal)
config: K=16 depth=2 ngram-n=3 reasoning=low max-ctx=40960 prefill-chunk=4096 max-new=256, greedy (temp=0)
method: SAME ring, SAME ~31.7k copy prompt, SAME config; only the transport differs (libp2p sidecar / per-node keys / no PSK  vs  raw-TCP + ChaCha+PSK). Decode tok/s tracks the per-round WAN round-trip (recv_ms_round); the n-gram nets g=6.69 tok per round.
headline: WARM libp2p decode on the copy task ran 18.4-36.0 tok/s across runs (peak 36.0 tok/s, recv 171ms/round) — 6.4-12.6x the old 2.86 COLD floor — with BIT-IDENTICAL output to raw-TCP and to itself; the spread is WAN RTT, not the transport.

parity.verdict: PARITY — at matched WAN round-trip latency the libp2p sidecar adds ~0 tax (1.1% of raw-TCP)
parity.note: the per-round latency (recv_ms_round) is the clean transport metric: 246ms (libp2p) vs 244ms (raw-TCP) — the localhost sidecar relays + Noise add a couple ms, swamped by the ~245ms cross-country WAN round-trip.

wan_jitter_caveat: ONE libp2p sample read 18.43 tok/s at recv=340ms — a slow-WAN MOMENT, not a transport tax: the same ring/transport later read 25.28 at recv=246ms. WAN RTT on a genuinely scattered MN-IL-MI-TX ring drifts ~245-340ms/round run-to-run; that spread (not the sidecar) dominates the tok/s variance. The matched-RTT comparison above is the honest A/B.
cold_floor_demolished: 2.86 tok/s (2026-06-23) was a COLD 3-stage / n-gram-accept=0 generative prompt — meaningless. The real WARM copy-task regime over libp2p is ~25 tok/s.
honest_notes: Absolute tok/s is bounded by the cross-country WAN round-trip (~245ms/round over 4 hops), NOT by the transport choice; a tighter/closer ring decodes faster.
Output is bit-identical across BOTH transports AND runs (greedy + n-gram is deterministic) — the transport is lossless: it changes speed only via WAN latency, never the result.
libp2p = per-node ed25519 keys, Noise-encrypted, NO SHARD_PSK — the permissionless transport, at raw-TCP parity.
